title: Multi-entropy in random tensor networks
authors: Miao Hu, Simon Lin, Ion Nechita
abstract: We study the evaluation of Rényi multi-entropies $S^{(q)}_n$ in Random Tensor Network (RTN) states in the large bond-dimension limit. For the case of Rényi index $n=2$ and arbitrary number of parties $q$, we prove that that multi-entropies are determined by minimal multiway cuts through the network. When the minimal multiway cut is degenerate, we characterize the full minimizer set via compatible families of minimal cuts and give a criterion for all minimizers to come from ordinary cut partitions. For $n=2$, this gives a natural generalization of the minimal cut description of bipartite entanglement to multipartite systems with arbitrarily many parties. For the case of integer $n>2$, we show that the minimal multiway cut conjecture is in general \emph{not true} by providing explicit counter examples for both the single random tensor and for the network built from isometric tilings. We discuss the implication for our results on the multipartite entanglement structures in RTN and holography.
summary: This work analyzes multi-partite entanglement in Random Tensor Networks by comparing generalized multi-entropies to minimal multiway cuts. While the conjecture holds for the simplest case (n=2), the authors prove it fails for higher Rényi indices (n>2) by identifying replica-symmetry-breaking saddle points that yield lower energy values. This has significant implications for applying geometric intuition to complex quantum entanglement structures.
main_problem: The paper investigates the relationship between Rényi multi-entropies in Random Tensor Network (RTN) states and the area of minimal multiway cuts in the large bond-dimension limit.
main_result: The multi-entropy equals the minimal multiway cut area only for the specific case of the Rényi index n=2; for n>2, the conjecture fails due to the existence of replica-symmetry-breaking saddle points.
method: The analysis uses saddle-point approximations derived from minimizing a free energy functional defined over permutations, relating it to geometric minimal cuts.
model_or_system: The model is Random Tensor Networks (RTNs) defined on graphs, which are used to simulate quantum states exhibiting multipartite entanglement, drawing analogies to the Ryu–Takayanagi formula in holography.
key_observables: Rényi multi-entropies $S^{(q)}_n$, the free energy $F(g)$, and the area of minimal multiway cuts $A(\{\Gamma_k\})$.
important_parameters: The Rényi index $n$, the number of parties $q$, and the bond dimension $\chi$ (taken to infinity).
main_assumptions: The analysis heavily relies on the replica trick and saddle-point approximations, which are shown to break down for $n>2$ due to replica symmetry breaking.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the geometric dualities, comparing entanglement entropy to minimal surfaces and proposing a dual relationship between multi-entropy and minimal multiway cuts.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the conjecture for $n=2$ (where it holds), then providing explicit counterexamples for $n>2$ by analyzing the free energy minimization, and finally characterizing the structure of the true minimizers using RSB elements.
why_interesting: It provides a deep, mathematically rigorous understanding of how entanglement measures (multi-entropies) relate to geometric structures (minimal cuts) in quantum many-body systems, which is crucial for understanding holographic duality.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: We tile the hyperbolic disk isometrically using a graph. Each dark blue node represents a random tensor and the connecting edges indicate tensor contractions. The random tensor network defines a state on the Hilbert space supported on the boundary, represented here by dangling legs. (a) The geometric dual of the entanglement entropy: The entropy of region A is proportional to the area of the minimal surface (black dashed) that divides A and its complement ¯A. (b) The proposed geometric dual for the multi-entropy. Here we take q = 3 as an example. The tripartite multi-entropy between region A : B : C is proportional to the area of the minimal multiway cut (also black dashed) that...
